steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank separates two independent clauses: 'the observatory sites... number nearly five [BLANK]' and 'the radars track mesospheric winds and other geospace phenomena.' Two independent clauses require a period, semicolon, or coordinating conjunction — not a comma alone.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘dozen, which are located’: places only a comma between the two independent clauses — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘dozen. Located’: uses a period to end the first sentence, then opens the second with the participial phrase ‘Located across the globe,’ which correctly modifies ‘the radars.’ Grammatically sound. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘dozen, located’: the participial phrase ‘located across the globe’ is only a modifier, so it does not separate the two independent clauses; the result is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘dozen located’: inserts a relative clause but still connects the two independent clauses with only a comma — another comma splice. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
